mitre_attack: T1530 - Data from Cloud Storage Object
T1213.003 - Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
cwe: CWE-732 - Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
CWE-668 - Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

threat.title: AWS S3 Bucket Public Exposure via ACL and Bucket Policy Misconfiguration
threat.description: Misconfigured Amazon S3 bucket permissions allow unauthorized public access to sensitive data through overly permissive Access Control Lists (ACLs), bucket policies, or disabled Block Public Access settings. This is one of the most common cloud security vulnerabilities, resulting in massive data breaches affecting millions of users and leading to regulatory fines exceeding hundreds of millions of dollars.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker uses automated scanning tools (e.g., S3Scanner, Bucket Stream) to discover publicly accessible S3 buckets by enumerating common naming patterns or monitoring DNS/certificate transparency logs
2. Identifies misconfigured bucket with public read permissions via overly permissive ACL (e.g., 'AllUsers' or 'AuthenticatedUsers' grants) or bucket policy with 's3:GetObject' allowed for Principal '*'
3. Lists bucket contents using AWS CLI: `aws s3 ls s3://target-bucket --no-sign-request`
4. Downloads sensitive files containing customer PII, credentials, API keys, database backups, or proprietary business data
5. Exfiltrates data to attacker-controlled infrastructure for exploitation, sale on dark web, or extortion
6. May also upload malicious files to writable buckets to host malware, deface applications, or inject supply chain attacks
prerequisites: S3 bucket has public read or write permissions enabled via ACL or bucket policy
Block Public Access settings are disabled or not configured at account or bucket level
Bucket contains sensitive data (PII, credentials, backups, intellectual property)
No encryption at rest or weak IAM policies allowing overly broad access
Missing CloudTrail logging or S3 access logging for detection

cve_references.description: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource - The software specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors
Improper Access Control - The software does not restrict or incorrectly restricts access to a resource from an unauthorized actor

real_world_breaches.impact: 106 million customers affected (100M US, 6M Canada); 140,000 Social Security numbers and 80,000 bank account numbers exposed; $80M regulatory fine from OCC, $190M customer lawsuit settlement; attackers exploited misconfigured WAF to access IAM role with excessive S3 permissions
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in misconfigured web application firewall leading to IAM role credential theft, which provided unauthorized access to S3 buckets containing sensitive customer data

real_world_breaches.incident: Twilio TaskRouter SDK S3 Bucket Exposure
real_world_breaches.impact: TaskRouter JavaScript SDK v1.20 compromised with malicious code; S3 bucket had public write permissions since 2015; attackers injected Magecart-style malvertising code; no customer data breach confirmed but supply chain attack vector exposed
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Publicly writable S3 bucket hosting production JavaScript SDK since 2015 due to misconfigured access policy that was not properly reset after troubleshooting

real_world_breaches.incident: Facebook/Instagram Third-Party App Data Exposure (Cultura Colectiva & At The Pool)
real_world_breaches.impact: 540+ million Facebook user records exposed (146GB dataset); 22,000 plaintext passwords from 'At The Pool' app; data included comments, likes, reactions, account names, FB IDs, email addresses, and relationship status stored without password protection
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Two third-party app developers stored Facebook user data in publicly accessible S3 buckets configured with public read permissions and no authentication requirements

real_world_breaches.impact: 1.5 million files (approximately 3TB) of security company data exposed without authentication; included employee and customer information, alarm codes, and security system configurations
real_world_breaches.root_cause: S3 bucket left open without any authentication requirements due to missing Block Public Access settings and overly permissive bucket policy

bug_bounty_reports.summary: AWS S3 bucket writeable for authenticated AWS users - any authenticated AWS user could write to the bucket without read access, enabling potential data injection attacks

security_research.title: The State of S3 Bucket Security in 2023
security_research.key_finding: 31% of S3 buckets remain publicly accessible, with misconfigurations being the leading cause of cloud data breaches

security_research.title: Cloud Security Report: AWS Misconfiguration Analysis
security_research.key_finding: S3 bucket misconfigurations account for 70% of critical cloud security findings, with public read access being the most common issue

security_research.title: A Systematic Analysis of the Capital One Data Breach: Critical Lessons Learned
security_research.key_finding: Capital One breach demonstrated cascading failure of least privilege principle: SSRF vulnerability → overly permissive IAM role → unrestricted S3 access

mitigations.title: Enable S3 Block Public Access at Account and Bucket Level
mitigations.description: AWS S3 Block Public Access provides four settings that work as a centralized control to prevent public access to S3 buckets and objects regardless of how they were created. This should be enabled by default at the account level and only selectively disabled for specific buckets with documented business justification.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Block Public Access at AWS Account Level (applies to all buckets)
aws s3control put-public-access-block \
  --account-id $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text) \
  --public-access-block-configuration \
    BlockPublicAcls=true,\
    IgnorePublicAcls=true,\
    BlockPublicPolicy=true,\
    RestrictPublicBuckets=true

echo "✓ Account-level Block Public Access enabled"

# Enable Block Public Access for specific bucket
BUCKET_NAME="my-sensitive-data-bucket"

aws s3api put-public-access-block \
  --bucket "$BUCKET_NAME" \
  --public-access-block-configuration \
    BlockPublicAcls=true,\
    IgnorePublicAcls=true,\
    BlockPublicPolicy=true,\
    RestrictPublicBuckets=true

echo "✓ Bucket-level Block Public Access enabled for $BUCKET_NAME"

# Verify configuration
aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket "$BUCKET_NAME"

# Use AWS CloudFormation for Infrastructure as Code
cat > s3-block-public-access.yaml << 'EOF'
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'S3 Bucket with Block Public Access Enabled'

Resources:
  SecureBucket:
    Type: 'AWS::S3::Bucket'
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-secure-bucket'
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: 'AES256'
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      LoggingConfiguration:
        DestinationBucketName: !Ref LoggingBucket
        LogFilePrefix: 'access-logs/'
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Production
        - Key: DataClassification
          Value: Confidential

  LoggingBucket:
    Type: 'AWS::S3::Bucket'
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-logs'
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldLogs
            Status: Enabled
            ExpirationInDays: 90
EOF

echo "✓ CloudFormation template created"
aws cloudformation deploy --template-file s3-block-public-access.yaml --stack-name secure-s3-stack

iso27001_controls: A.9.1.2 - Access to networks and network services
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Verify Block Public Access is enabled at account level using AWS CLI or console
Test that attempts to add public ACLs or policies are rejected
Use AWS Config rule s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited to monitor compliance
Validate with AWS Trusted Advisor S3 Bucket Permissions check
Run AWS Access Analyzer to identify any remaining public buckets

mitigations.title: Implement Least Privilege S3 Bucket Policies with IAM Conditions
mitigations.description: Replace overly permissive bucket policies with restrictive IAM-based access control using conditions for IP ranges, VPC endpoints, MFA, and time-based restrictions. Remove wildcard (*) principals and implement explicit deny statements for public access.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited
PR.AC-4 - Access permissions and authorizations are managed
validation_steps: Use IAM Policy Simulator to test access from unauthorized principals
Verify MFA enforcement by attempting sensitive operations without MFA
Test VPC endpoint restrictions from EC2 instances outside the VPC
Use AWS Access Analyzer to check for external access paths
Review CloudTrail logs for denied S3 API calls to validate policy enforcement

mitigations.title: Deploy AWS Access Analyzer for S3 and Automated Remediation
mitigations.description: Use AWS Access Analyzer to continuously monitor S3 buckets for external access and integrate with AWS Lambda for automated remediation of public bucket exposures. Implement detective controls and automated response workflows.

code_example.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Lambda function to automatically remediate public S3 buckets
# Triggered by EventBridge rule monitoring AWS Access Analyzer findings

import json
import boto3
import os
from datetime import datetime

s3_client = boto3.client('s3')
sns_client = boto3.client('sns')
access_analyzer = boto3.client('accessanalyzer')

SNS_TOPIC_ARN = os.environ['SNS_TOPIC_ARN']
AUTO_REMEDIATE = os.environ.get('AUTO_REMEDIATE', 'true').lower() == 'true'

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """
    Automatically remediate public S3 buckets detected by Access Analyzer
    """
    print(f"Event received: {json.dumps(event)}")
    
    # Parse Access Analyzer finding
    detail = event.get('detail', {})
    finding_type = detail.get('findingType', '')
    resource_type = detail.get('resourceType', '')
    
    if resource_type != 'AWS::S3::Bucket':
        print(f"Skipping non-S3 resource: {resource_type}")
        return
    
    if finding_type != 'ExternalAccess':
        print(f"Skipping non-external access finding: {finding_type}")
        return
    
    # Extract bucket ARN and name
    resource_arn = detail.get('resource', '')
    bucket_name = resource_arn.split(':')[-1]
    finding_id = detail.get('id', '')
    principal = detail.get('principal', {})
    
    print(f"Public S3 bucket detected: {bucket_name}")
    print(f"Finding ID: {finding_id}")
    print(f"External principal: {principal}")
    
    # Get current bucket configuration
    try:
        public_access_block = s3_client.get_public_access_block(Bucket=bucket_name)
        current_config = public_access_block['PublicAccessBlockConfiguration']
    except s3_client.exceptions.NoSuchPublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        current_config = None
    
    # Prepare remediation actions
    actions_taken = []
    
    if AUTO_REMEDIATE:
        # Enable Block Public Access
        try:
            s3_client.put_public_access_block(
                Bucket=bucket_name,
                PublicAccessBlockConfiguration={
                    'BlockPublicAcls': True,
                    'IgnorePublicAcls': True,
                    'BlockPublicPolicy': True,
                    'RestrictPublicBuckets': True
                }
            )
            actions_taken.append('Enabled Block Public Access')
            print(f"✓ Block Public Access enabled for {bucket_name}")
        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Failed to enable Block Public Access: {str(e)}"
            actions_taken.append(error_msg)
            print(error_msg)
        
        # Check and remediate bucket ACL
        try:
            acl = s3_client.get_bucket_acl(Bucket=bucket_name)
            public_grants = [g for g in acl['Grants'] 
                           if g['Grantee'].get('Type') == 'Group' and 
                              ('AllUsers' in g['Grantee'].get('URI', '') or 
                               'AuthenticatedUsers' in g['Grantee'].get('URI', ''))]
            
            if public_grants:
                # Remove public ACL grants (set to private)
                s3_client.put_bucket_acl(Bucket=bucket_name, ACL='private')
                actions_taken.append(f"Removed {len(public_grants)} public ACL grants")
                print(f"✓ Public ACL grants removed from {bucket_name}")
        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Failed to remediate ACL: {str(e)}"
            actions_taken.append(error_msg)
            print(error_msg)
        
        # Review bucket policy for public access
        try:
            policy = s3_client.get_bucket_policy(Bucket=bucket_name)
            policy_doc = json.loads(policy['Policy'])
            
            # Check for wildcard principals
            has_public_policy = False
            for statement in policy_doc.get('Statement', []):
                principal = statement.get('Principal', {})
                if principal == '*' or principal == {'AWS': '*'}:
                    has_public_policy = True
                    break
            
            if has_public_policy:
                actions_taken.append('WARNING: Bucket policy contains wildcard principal - manual review required')
                print(f"⚠ Bucket policy for {bucket_name} requires manual review")
        except s3_client.exceptions.NoSuchBucketPolicy:
            actions_taken.append('No bucket policy present')
        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Failed to check bucket policy: {str(e)}"
            actions_taken.append(error_msg)
            print(error_msg)
    
    # Send SNS notification
    notification = {
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'severity': 'CRITICAL',
        'finding_type': finding_type,
        'bucket_name': bucket_name,
        'finding_id': finding_id,
        'external_principal': principal,
        'auto_remediate_enabled': AUTO_REMEDIATE,
        'actions_taken': actions_taken,
        'current_config': current_config,
        'recommendation': 'Review bucket access requirements and validate remediation',
        'documentation': 'https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-control-block-public-access.html'
    }
    
    try:
        sns_client.publish(
            TopicArn=SNS_TOPIC_ARN,
            Subject=f'[CRITICAL] Public S3 Bucket Detected: {bucket_name}',
            Message=json.dumps(notification, indent=2)
        )
        print(f"✓ SNS notification sent")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Failed to send SNS notification: {str(e)}")
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps(notification)
    }

# EventBridge Rule (CloudFormation template)
"""
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Automated S3 Public Bucket Remediation'

Resources:
  AccessAnalyzer:
    Type: 'AWS::AccessAnalyzer::Analyzer'
    Properties:
      AnalyzerName: 'S3PublicAccessAnalyzer'
      Type: 'ACCOUNT'
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: SecurityAutomation
  
  RemediationFunction:
    Type: 'AWS::Lambda::Function'
    Properties:
      FunctionName: 'S3-Public-Bucket-Remediation'
      Runtime: python3.11
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          # Paste lambda function code here
      Environment:
        Variables:
          SNS_TOPIC_ARN: !Ref AlertTopic
          AUTO_REMEDIATE: 'true'
      Role: !GetAtt RemediationRole.Arn
      Timeout: 60
  
  EventRule:
    Type: 'AWS::Events::Rule'
    Properties:
      Name: 'S3-Public-Access-Analyzer-Findings'
      Description: 'Trigger remediation for public S3 buckets'
      EventPattern:
        source:
          - 'aws.access-analyzer'
        detail-type:
          - 'Access Analyzer Finding'
        detail:
          resourceType:
            - 'AWS::S3::Bucket'
          findingType:
            - 'ExternalAccess'
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt RemediationFunction.Arn
          Id: 'RemediationLambda'
  
  LambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: 'AWS::Lambda::Permission'
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref RemediationFunction
      Action: 'lambda:InvokeFunction'
      Principal: 'events.amazonaws.com'
      SourceArn: !GetAtt EventRule.Arn
  
  AlertTopic:
    Type: 'AWS::SNS::Topic'
    Properties:
      DisplayName: 'S3 Security Alerts'
      Subscription:
        - Endpoint: 'security-team@example.com'
          Protocol: email
  
  RemediationRole:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::Role'
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - 'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole'
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: S3RemediationPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 's3:PutPublicAccessBlock'
                  - 's3:GetPublicAccessBlock'
                  - 's3:GetBucketAcl'
                  - 's3:PutBucketAcl'
                  - 's3:GetBucketPolicy'
                  - 's3:ListAllMyBuckets'
                Resource: '*'
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 'sns:Publish'
                Resource: !Ref AlertTopic
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 'access-analyzer:ListFindings'
                  - 'access-analyzer:GetFinding'
                Resource: '*'
"""

tools.name: AWS Access Analyzer
tools.cost: $0.20 per policy analyzed; free for external access findings

iso27001_controls: A.16.1.4 - Assessment of and decision on information security events
A.16.1.5 - Response to information security incidents
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
DE.CM-7 - Monitoring for unauthorized personnel, connections, devices, and software is performed
RS.RP-1 - Response plan is executed during or after an incident
validation_steps: Deploy Access Analyzer and wait for initial scan completion (up to 30 minutes)
Create test bucket with public access to trigger finding
Verify EventBridge rule triggers Lambda function
Confirm Lambda successfully enables Block Public Access
Validate SNS notification delivery to security team
Review CloudWatch Logs for function execution details
Test manual override capability for legitimate public buckets

indicators: S3 GetObject API calls from unexpected or foreign IP addresses
Large volume of ListBucket operations from unknown AWS accounts
S3 PutBucketAcl or PutBucketPolicy operations making buckets public
Access denied errors followed by successful access after permission changes
Data exfiltration patterns: high volume GetObject requests in short time window
S3 PutObject operations from unauthorized sources to writable buckets

log_queries.query:
-- Detect S3 GetObject requests from public/non-corporate IP addresses
-- Run in Amazon Athena against CloudTrail logs table

SELECT 
  eventtime,
  eventname,
  sourceipaddress,
  useridentity.principalid,
  requestparameters,
  json_extract_scalar(requestparameters, '$.bucketName') as bucket_name,
  json_extract_scalar(requestparameters, '$.key') as object_key,
  errorcode,
  errormessage
FROM cloudtrail_logs
WHERE 
  eventname IN ('GetObject', 'ListBucket', 'GetBucketAcl', 'GetBucketPolicy')
  AND eventsource = 's3.amazonaws.com'
  AND year = '2026' 
  AND month = '01'
  -- Exclude corporate IP ranges
  AND sourceipaddress NOT LIKE '10.%'
  AND sourceipaddress NOT LIKE '172.16.%'
  AND sourceipaddress NOT LIKE '192.168.%'
  AND sourceipaddress NOT IN ('203.0.113.0', '198.51.100.0')
  -- Focus on unauthenticated or suspicious access
  AND (
    useridentity.principalid = 'ANONYMOUS_PRINCIPAL'
    OR useridentity.accountid != '123456789012'  -- Replace with your AWS account ID
  )
ORDER BY eventtime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

log_queries.siem: AWS CloudWatch Insights (CloudWatch Logs Insights Query Language)
log_queries.query:
# Detect bucket permission changes that enable public access
# Query CloudTrail logs ingested into CloudWatch Logs

fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.principalId, sourceIPAddress, requestParameters.bucketName, requestParameters.publicAccessBlockConfiguration
| filter eventSource = "s3.amazonaws.com"
| filter eventName in [
    "PutBucketAcl", 
    "PutBucketPolicy", 
    "DeletePublicAccessBlock",
    "PutPublicAccessBlock"
  ]
| filter (
    requestParameters.publicAccessBlockConfiguration.blockPublicAcls = false
    or requestParameters.publicAccessBlockConfiguration.blockPublicPolicy = false
    or eventName = "DeletePublicAccessBlock"
    or requestParameters.AccessControlPolicy.AccessControlList.Grant.Grantee.URI like "AllUsers"
  )
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

index=aws_cloudtrail sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail eventSource=s3.amazonaws.com
  (eventName=GetObject OR eventName=ListBucket OR eventName=GetBucketAcl)
  | where NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", sourceIPAddress) 
      AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", sourceIPAddress)
      AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16", sourceIPAddress)
  | eval bucket_name=spath(requestParameters, "bucketName")
  | eval object_key=spath(requestParameters, "key")
  | eval principal_type=spath(userIdentity, "type")
  | where principal_type="Anonymous" OR userIdentity.accountId!="123456789012"
  | stats count dc(sourceIPAddress) as unique_ips dc(object_key) as objects_accessed 
      by bucket_name, sourceIPAddress, userIdentity.principalId
  | where count > 100 OR objects_accessed > 50
  | sort -count
  | table _time, bucket_name, sourceIPAddress, userIdentity.principalId, count, objects_accessed, unique_ips

event.dataset: "aws.cloudtrail" 
AND event.action: ("GetObject" OR "ListBucket" OR "PutBucketAcl" OR "PutBucketPolicy" OR "DeletePublicAccessBlock")
AND event.provider: "s3.amazonaws.com"
AND (
  aws.cloudtrail.user_identity.type: "Anonymous" 
  OR aws.cloudtrail.user_identity.type: "AWSService"
)
AND NOT source.ip: (10.0.0.0/8 OR 172.16.0.0/12 OR 192.168.0.0/16)
| stats count by aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters.bucketName, source.ip, aws.cloudtrail.user_identity.principal_id

tools.name: AWS Access Analyzer
tools.usage: Automatically scans all S3 buckets and reports external access findings

tools.name: AWS Trusted Advisor
tools.usage: Checks S3 Bucket Permissions for public access (available in Business/Enterprise Support)

tools.name: AWS Config Rules
tools.usage: Use managed rules: s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited, s3-bucket-public-write-prohibited, s3-bucket-level-public-access-prohibited

tools.name: S3Scanner
tools.usage: Open-source tool to find publicly accessible S3 buckets: python3 s3scanner.py --bucket target-bucket

tools.name: CloudMapper
tools.usage: Visualize AWS environment and identify public S3 buckets: cloudmapper prepare && cloudmapper webserver

compliance: ISO 27001:2022 (A.8.9 - Configuration Management, A.9.1 - Access Control)
NIST CSF v1.1 (PR.AC-4, PR.DS-5, DE.CM-1, RS.AN-1)
SOC 2 Type II (CC6.1 - Logical Access Controls)
